message:
feat(recorder_storage): Cx-first trait default methods (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Add `_with_cx` default methods to the `RecorderStorage` trait.
This is a high-leverage slice: every current and future
RecorderStorage backend automatically gets Cx-first entry points
without touching its impl block (default methods delegate to the
non-cx methods after a pre-flight `cx.checkpoint()?`).

Parallel to tick 44-46's `WeztermInterface` trait extensions —
same pattern: default method provides a sensible baseline,
concrete backends can override when they want deeper cx
threading (e.g. a backend using `timeout_with_cx` internally
on its append path).

Additions (all strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `RecorderStorage::append_batch_with_cx(cx, req)` — default
    delegates to `append_batch` after a checkpoint.

  * `RecorderStorage::flush_with_cx(cx, mode)` — same pattern.

  * `RecorderStorage::read_checkpoint_with_cx(cx, consumer)` —
    same pattern.

  * `RecorderStorage::commit_checkpoint_with_cx(cx, checkpoint)`
    — same pattern.

  * `RecorderStorage::lag_metrics_with_cx(cx)` — same pattern.

`health()` deliberately has no cx sibling — it's a snapshot
accessor with no backing I/O on most backends (returns cached
`RecorderStorageHealth`). Adding cx there would be noise.

Error mapping: cancellation wraps into `RecorderStorageError::Io`
via `std::io::Error::other(...)` with "cancelled" prefix for
pattern-matching. Preserves the existing `Result<_,
RecorderStorageError>` signature so callers don't need to
handle a new error variant.

Impact: tick 67-71 recorder_migration Cx-first migrations
currently use `cx.checkpoint()?` between storage calls. With
this trait extension, they can be simplified to
`storage.append_batch_with_cx(cx, req).await` — same
cancellation seam, cleaner call site. That refactor is a
follow-on tick; landing the trait defaults first unblocks it.

Regression tests (2 new):

  * `trait_append_batch_with_cx_matches_legacy` — writes one
    event via `append_batch_with_cx`, then one via
    `append_batch`, asserts AppendResponse parity: cx-first
    response has ordinal=0, legacy has ordinal=1, and legacy
    byte_offset > cx byte_offset. Proves the Cx-first default
    didn't accidentally skip, reorder, or duplicate the write.

  * `trait_checkpoint_cx_variants_round_trip` — writes a
    checkpoint via `commit_checkpoint_with_cx`, reads it back
    via `read_checkpoint_with_cx`, asserts the round-trip
    preserved all fields (consumer, ordinal, byte_offset,
    schema_version).

Both tests use `AppendLogRecorderStorage` as the concrete
backend, which uses the trait defaults (doesn't override). So
the tests specifically exercise the default-method path.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * recorder_storage:: — 101 passed / 0 failed (tick 73: 99 ->
    tick 74: 101, +2 new default-method parity tests)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (33.94s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
